steps.title: Spot the central contrast
steps.content: The passage sets up a contrast: Crowley isn't commercially famous like major American writers, yet has had influential champions (Bloom read Little, Big 46 times and called him universally relevant). This juxtaposition—commercial obscurity vs. critical acclaim—signals the passage's topic.

steps.title: Match the contrast to the answer
steps.content:
The passage is fundamentally about critical reception—how Crowley's work has been received by important literary figures.

The other three choices miss this focus: they involve comparisons, analyses, and explanations that the passage doesn't provide.

That matches Choice D — The reception of the work of John Crowley..
